senses.text: "immoderate eating and drinking," 1520s, from Late Latin ingurgitationem (nominative ingurgitatio), noun of action from past participle stem of Latin ingurgitare "plunge into, gorge," from in- "in, into" (see in- (2)) + gurgitare "to engulf," from gurges "whirlpool, gorge" (see gurges).
